findings.description: Officials' names (PAT McCRORY/Governor, DONALD R. VAN DER VAART/Secretary, TRACY DAVIS/Director) are duplicated on page 1. They appear once in the letterhead header area and then again as inline body text below a colored banner bar before the MEMORANDUM heading. Footer address text also appears between the header area and the memo body content.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows officials' names once in the upper-right header. HTML page 1 shows them first in the header area, then footer text ('State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality...'), then a colored bar, then officials' names listed again as body text, then the MEMORANDUM heading.

findings.description: Repeated footer and header content inserted between page-equivalent sections disrupts reading flow. Between the end of page 1 content (outline I–IV) and the start of page 2 content (Section I History/Background), the HTML inserts footer address text and a header banner bar with officials' names.
findings.evidence: PDF flows directly from the outline on page 1 to Section I on page 2 with only a page break. HTML interposes footer text and a colored header banner with officials' names between these sections, creating an interruption for a continuous reader.
